title.description: Short, clear title for the simulation. Should indicate the physics concept. Examples: "Toss Ball", "Two Boxes Collision", "Pendulum Motion"

description.description: Brief educational description for students explaining what they can learn or observe. Keep it engaging.

environment.description: Environment settings. Controls units, scale, boundaries, and gravity. Uses real-world physics coordinates: origin at bottom-left, Y increases upward.

walls.description: Array of walls to include. Options: "left", "right", "top", "bottom". Empty array [] = no walls (objects can exit canvas). Use walls to contain objects or create bounce surfaces.

gravity.description: Gravity acceleration in units/s² (downward). Default: 9.8 for Earth gravity in m/s². Set to 0 for zero-gravity. For cm/s² use 980.

unit.description: Unit of measurement for all positions, velocities, and sizes. Default: "m" (meters). Options: "m", "cm", "km", "ft", "in".

pixelsPerUnit.description: Scale factor: how many pixels equal one unit. The simulation canvas is 800×600 pixels, so the SI canvas size is (800/pixelsPerUnit) × (600/pixelsPerUnit). Pick this value so the largest object is roughly 10–25% of the smaller canvas dimension.

physicsEngine.description: Which physics engine powers the simulation. "matter" uses Matter.js. "rapier" uses Rapier (WASM, SI-native, deterministic, default). "planck" uses Planck.js (pure JS port of Box2D, SI-native). Existing configs without this field use rapier.

objects.description: Array of physics objects in the simulation. 1-3 objects recommended. Each object's collider shape and visual sprite both come from its `svg` manifest entry, scaled to width × height.

items.description: A physics object in the simulation. Carries position, bounding-box size, an SVG manifest name (which drives both visual and collider), and optional physics properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for this object (e.g., "ball", "boxA", "platform"). Used by controls, outputs, and graphs to reference this object.

x.description: Initial X position of the object's center, in configured units. With default settings (pixelsPerUnit=10, unit="m"), canvas is 80m wide. X=0 is left edge.

y.description: Initial Y position of the object's center, in configured units. With default settings, canvas is 60m tall. Y=0 is bottom edge, Y increases upward.

width.description: Bounding-box width in configured units. The actual collider shape (rectangle, circle, or convex hull) is looked up from the SVG manifest by `svg` and scaled into this box.

height.description: Bounding-box height in configured units. The actual collider shape is looked up from the SVG manifest by `svg` and scaled into this box.

svg.description: Name of a renderable from public/renderables/manifest.json (e.g., "soccer_ball", "brick_block", "boat"). Drives both the visual sprite and the physical collider shape, scaled to width × height.

velocity.description: Initial linear velocity {x, y} in units/second. Positive Y = upward motion. Typical range: -30 to 30 m/s.

x.description: X component. For velocity: positive = rightward, negative = leftward. For position: depends on pixelsPerUnit scale.

y.description: Y component. For velocity: positive = downward, negative = upward. For position: Y increases upward from origin at bottom-left.

acceleration.description: Initial linear acceleration {x, y} in units/s². Usually not set directly; gravity provides Y acceleration.

x.description: X component. For velocity: positive = rightward, negative = leftward. For position: depends on pixelsPerUnit scale.

y.description: Y component. For velocity: positive = downward, negative = upward. For position: Y increases upward from origin at bottom-left.

restitution.description: Bounciness (0-1). 0 = no bounce, 1 = perfect bounce, 0.8 = realistic. Default: 0.8

frictionAir.description: Air resistance (0-1). 0 = no drag, 0.01-0.05 = light damping, 0.1 = high drag. Default: 0

friction.description: Surface friction (0-1). Affects sliding against other objects. Default: 0.1

frictionStatic.description: Static surface friction (0-10). As in a Coulomb friction model, static friction affects friction resistance when an object is at rest. Default: 0.5

inertia.description: inertia is the second moment of area in two dimensions, affects rotation. Set to 1e10 for to prevent body rotation. Default: 0

isStatic.description: If true, object is immovable (good for floors, walls, platforms). Default: false

mass.description: Mass of the object in kg. Default: 1

angularVelocity.description: Initial angular velocity in radians/second. Default: 0

showForceArrows.description: If true, draw arrows on this object showing the net force from the physics engine. Default: false

controls.description: Interactive controls (sliders, toggles) for students to adjust parameters. Include at least one for interactivity.

items.description: Interactive control for students to adjust simulation parameters. Sliders for numeric values, toggles for booleans.

oneOf.description: Slider control for adjusting numeric properties. Values are in real-world units (meters, etc.) with Y-up coordinates.

label.description: Display label shown to users (e.g., "Initial Velocity", "Box A Speed")

targetObj.description: ID of the object to control (must match an object's id)

min.description: Minimum slider value in configured units. Velocity: typically -30 to 30 m/s. Position X: 0 to canvas width. Position Y: 0 to canvas height.

max.description: Maximum slider value in configured units. With default settings: Position X max ~80m, Position Y max ~60m.

step.description: Slider increment in configured units. Use 0.01 for fine control, 0.1 for coarse control.

defaultValue.description: Initial value when simulation loads in configured units. Should match the object's initial property value.

targetObj.description: ID of the object to control (must match an object's id)

outputs.description: Real-time value displays. Group by object. Show velocity, acceleration, position as relevant.

items.description: A group of output values, typically for one object. Groups values visually.

values.description: Array of values to display in this group

items.description: A single value to display in real-time. Values are automatically converted to real-world units.

targetObj.description: ID of the object to read from (must match an object's id)

unit.description: Unit label for display (e.g., "m/s", "m/s²", "m"). Will use the configured unit type. Leave blank to auto-generate based on property and environment unit.

graphs.description: Time-series graphs to visualize property changes. Great for comparing related quantities.

items.description: Graph configuration. Currently supports line graphs for time-series data.

oneOf.description: Time-series line graph. X-axis is time, Y-axis shows property values in real-world units. Great for comparing velocity vs acceleration.

title.description: Graph title (e.g., "Velocity Over Time", "Position vs Time")

yAxisRange.description: Y-axis range in configured units. X-axis is always time in seconds.

min.description: Minimum Y-axis value in configured units (e.g., -2 for -2 m/s). Set to encompass expected values with padding.

max.description: Maximum Y-axis value in configured units (e.g., 2 for 2 m/s). Set to encompass expected values with padding.

yAxisLabel.description: Y-axis label with units (e.g., "Velocity (m/s)", "Position (m)"). If not provided, defaults to "Value".

lines.description: Array of lines to plot. Use contrasting colors for multiple lines.

items.description: Configuration for a single line on a graph. Values are automatically converted to real-world units.

label.description: Line label for the legend (e.g., "Velocity Y", "Box A Position")

targetObj.description: ID of the object to track (must match an object's id)
